title: Merge Conflicts
content:
# HTML-CSS-Git Interview Prep

## Q1: How does Git compute three-way merges under the hood and resolve conflicts?

### Standard Answer
Git locates the lowest common ancestor (LCA) commit of the two branches. It computes diffs from the LCA to the tip of Branch A, and from the LCA to the tip of Branch B. If a file changed in Branch A but remained unchanged in Branch B, Git applies the change automatically. If the same file line was modified in both branches with conflicting edits, Git halts the merge, marks conflict zones inside the file boundaries, and requests manual developer resolution before commit.


## Q2: How does the system handle concurrent throughput anomalies under Merge Conflicts?

### Standard Answer
The system uses dynamic resource pools and queue-backoff throttles to maintain throughput boundaries, avoiding thread starvation.
